key_insight: Additive singular vector basis functions for tetrahedral FEM can directly capture electromagnetic field singularities in 3D complex geometries, greatly improving accuracy and efficiency.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical simulation of highly singular EM devices such as superconducting qubits at far lower runtime and memory cost than standard FEM basis functions.
method: Developed singular vector basis functions for tetrahedra that are additive to standard vector FEM bases.
Constructed functions to be singular with respect to individual nodes and edges, making them adaptable to complex geometries.
Provided higher-order interpolatory variants for improved singular-field approximation.
evidence_signal: In the superconducting qubit example, the approach matched results from Ansys HFSS while reducing runtime from 21.27 hours to 196 seconds and peak memory from 6.23 TB to 27.24 GB.
watch_item: These functions target geometry-dependent singular features, so correct identification and implementation of singular nodes/edges is critical for robust use.
paper_title: Singular Vector Finite Element Basis Functions for Tetrahedra in Complex Electromagnetic Geometries
